method_atom: A scheme for reversibly storing and retrieving many parallel independent channels (modes) of a signal in a single distributed medium composed of a large collection of interacting units, exploiting the medium's many internal degrees of freedom to capture not just per-channel amplitudes but the full joint state across channels, including coherent superpositions and correlations between channels. Because storage runs over all channels in parallel, the information capacity scales with the number of independent modes and exceeds that of any single-channel store.
object_atom: The quantum state of light forming an optical image with many transverse spatial modes (including multimode superpositions and entangled photon states), written into and read back out of a large ensemble of atoms serving as a light-matter quantum interface and quantum memory.
brief_reason: Transferable is the parallel multi-channel storage of a signal's full joint state (superpositions and cross-channel correlations) in a many-unit collective medium with capacity scaling in the number of modes; domain-specific is that the signal is quantum light/images and the medium is a multi-atom ensemble.
